agent_raw: The evaluation is methodologically sound. The missingness indicators are submission-time features confirmed by a feature-generation timestamp audit — they are not post-decision or post-outcome proxies. An applicant who submits without income documentation and a bureau query that returns a thin-file code both reflect the applicant's state at application submission, before any underwriting decision. These are legitimate predictive signals available at scoring time. The future-quarter holdout evaluates the frozen model on new applications using exactly the submission-time feature set. The claim is correctly restricted to the same underwriting workflow and document-collection process. Workflow dependence is disclosed as a scope limitation. The evaluation is valid.
